title: BioMedical Knowledge Graph
summary: A comprehensive biomedical knowledge graph with 20 node types and 30 relationship types, covering drugs, diseases, genes, proteins, pathways, clinical trials, and more. Built for GraphRAG-powered biomedical question answering.

chunks.text: BioMedical Knowledge Graph containing 212 entities across 20 node types (Drug, Disease, Gene, Protein, Biomarker, ClinicalTrial, Researcher, Institution, AdverseEvent, ResearchPaper, Pathway, Anatomy, CellType, BiologicalProcess, MolecularFunction, Entity, Exposure, Phenotype, Cluster, ClusterSummary) connected by 423 relationships of 30 types.

therapeutic_relevance: High; summary_text: Cancer drugs primarily target receptor tyrosine kinases and immune checkpoints
therapeutic_relevance: High; summary_text: Neurodegeneration involves amyloid processing, tau phosphorylation, and mitochondrial dysfunction
therapeutic_relevance: High; summary_text: Immune checkpoint inhibitors block PD-1/PD-L1 axis to restore anti-tumor immunity
therapeutic_relevance: High; summary_text: Metabolic syndrome genes converge on insulin signaling and lipid metabolism pathways
therapeutic_relevance: High; summary_text: Kinase inhibitors target BCR-ABL, EGFR, and BRAF with high selectivity
therapeutic_relevance: Medium; summary_text: Inflammatory genes regulate NF-kB signaling and cytokine production
therapeutic_relevance: High; summary_text: Apoptosis regulation involves BCL-2 family members and caspase cascades
therapeutic_relevance: Medium; summary_text: Angiogenesis module centers on VEGF signaling and endothelial cell proliferation
